formations_blocks: Belgium sustained early attacking pressure / onslaught
Iran attempted to counter after defending deep spells
adjustments: Lukaku introduced into aerial/central attacking duels on set pieces and crosses
Trossard drifting/wriggling down the left to create cut-backs
key_matchups: De Cuyper vs Taremi (first-contact defending on the corner)
phases.summary: Belgium began strongly, pinning Iran back with sustained pressure, quick regains, and repeated entries into the box. Their attacks came through left-sided dribbles and cut-backs as well as crosses and shots from De Bruyne, while Iran were largely reduced to defensive interventions and the occasional counter attempt.

formations_blocks: Belgium attacking against a compact defensive block
adjustments: Belgium using quick combination play to deconstruct the low block
Meunier inverting to create an extra man on the last line
key_matchups: Lukaku attacking through balls and crosses
phases.summary: Belgium dominate the ball and repeatedly try to break down Iran's low block with quick combination play, although the final pass is often overhit. Iran survive a scare after a disallowed Taremi goal, then threaten from set pieces and transitions, while Belgium respond by pushing Meunier inside to add an extra body on the last line.

There is no real tactical analysis in this phase beyond the scoreline at the break: Belgium and Iran are level at 0-0. The only football-specific comment is a brief discussion of Lukaku's disciplinary risk and whether he should be withdrawn at half-time, but no formations, block structure, or matchup detail is given.

formations_blocks: Iran defensive shape implied to be adjusted after the substitution, possibly shifting from a back five to a four-at-the-back structure
Belgium attacking in sustained possession/territorial pressure from wide areas and set pieces
adjustments: Iran replace Hardani with Jahanbakhsh at 46', with the commentary explicitly suggesting this may signal a move to four at the back
Belgium continue to target the right side and the back post from free kicks and corners through De Bruyne
Trossard moves infield to link with De Bruyne, indicating Belgium’s left-sided attacker operating more centrally
key_matchups: Trossard vs the Iran defender who clipped him, leading to a free-kick deep in Iran’s half
De Bruyne vs Iran’s defensive set-piece coverage, repeatedly delivering into the box
Saelemaekers at the back post arriving against Iran’s retreating defenders on De Bruyne’s cross
phases.summary:
The opening of the second half suggests Iran may have altered their structure, with the substitution of Hardani for Jahanbakhsh possibly indicating a switch to a back four. Belgium respond by leaning on territorial pressure and set pieces, with De Bruyne repeatedly delivering dangerous balls and Trossard and Saelemaekers finding half-chances in the box.

The main tactical pattern is Belgium probing from the right and via dead balls, while Trossard drifts inside to connect play with De Bruyne. Iran’s defensive block is under strain, but the commentary only directly confirms their shape change as a possibility rather than a certainty.

formations_blocks: Iran are chasing the game with an attacking posture
adjustments: Iran make attacking substitutions, bringing on Moghanloo for Ghoddos and later Hosseinzadeh for Ezatolahi
Iran go from a more balanced shape to an all-out attacking setup in search of a winner/qualifier
Belgium respond by protecting space and absorbing pressure
key_matchups: Ezatolahi vs Courtois on the long-range shot that the goalkeeper cannot hold
De Bruyne vs Iran's box-defending line on the wide free-kick and later the build-up to Castagne's cross
Tielemans vs Iran's defensive recovery on the low cross from Castagne
phases.summary: This phase turns into an end-to-end, crunch-time tactical battle after the red card, with Iran pushing men forward and Belgium retreating into a more conservative stance. Iran’s substitutions reinforce the chase, but that ambition also opens space for Belgium, who threaten through De Bruyne-led service wide and into the box. The clearest chance comes from Ezatolahi’s long-ranger, while Belgium’s best late opening is Castagne’s low cross that Tielemans can only glance away.
